meta.title: Jonathan Brownstein — Business Analysis Manager, Global Payment Net…
meta.description: Senior business analytics leader with 15+ years driving strategic decisions through quantitative analysis in regulated financial services. Seven years inside the Data Analytics Office at Hannover Reinsurance Company lead

hero.badge: Business Analysis Manager, Global Payment Net…
hero.description: Senior business analytics leader with 15+ years driving strategic decisions through quantitative analysis in regulated financial services. Seven years inside the Data Analytics Office at Hannover Reinsurance Company leading the analytics, predictive models, and executive reporting the C-suite uses to make decisions. Comfortable running multiple concurrent initiatives end to end, leading teams of analysts and engineers, and partnering across finance, IT, risk, compliance, and outside vendors to ship audit-ready work on a deadline. Quantitative degree path (MS Economics, Applied Econometrics certificate), FLMI from LOMA, and a track record that includes the classification ML rebuild of a regulated reporting workflow recognized by the company's former CEO and the automated ARIMA forecasting platform for Net Amount at Risk.

highlights.label: Business strategy development and decision support
Predictive modeling (classification ML, ARIMA time-series forecasting, anomaly and outlier detection)

items.title: Senior Data Analyst III
items.context: Technical lead and senior analytics owner inside the Data Analytics Office
items.intro: Senior individual contributor and technical lead inside the Data Analytics Office of a global life and health reinsurer. Lead the strategic analytics, predictive modeling, and executive reporting the actuarial, valuation, finance, and risk teams rely on, lead teams of analysts and engineers on multiple concurrent initiatives, and manage the outside vendors and consultants who support delivery.

subsections.title: Strategic Analytics and Decision Support
bullets: Led the classification machine learning rebuild of the Same-As-Link (SAL) regulated reporting workflow, the firm's record-to-standard-identifier mapping problem. Accuracy, consistency, and audit readiness all improved measurably, and the company's former CEO recognized the result.

Designed and deployed an automated ARIMA forecasting platform for Net Amount at Risk (NAR) on a scheduled production pipeline. Surfaces anomalies and movement before period close so actuarial and finance leaders can react in time.

Built Probability Density Function outlier-detection frameworks across platforms processing more than a trillion records, catching attribute drift in production data before it reaches downstream reports and decision-makers.

Designed the firm's first valuation-extract comparison architecture, a repeatable framework for period-over-period movement analysis across time periods and source systems that other teams have since extended.

subsections.title: Executive Reporting and Cross-Functional Partnership
bullets: Build the Power BI, Tableau, and SSRS dashboards actuarial leadership, finance, and the C-suite use to read KPIs and movements. Translating model output and complex data movements into a clean executive narrative is a core part of the role.

Partner across actuarial, valuation, finance, risk, IT, BI, and compliance to turn business questions into governed analytics deliverables on a deadline. Audit-ready delivery in a regulated reinsurance environment is the operating standard.

Select and manage the outside vendors and consultants who support analytics and reporting delivery. Own scope, timelines, and quality end to end, and partner with governance and compliance to keep the work defensible.

subsections.title: Technical Leadership and Talent Development
bullets: Lead teams of analysts and engineers on the initiatives above, running architecture and design review, code review, and delivery checkpoints across concurrent projects.
Mentor and develop analysts and engineers on Databricks, Spark, SQL, and Python analytics. Comfortable stepping into formal people management on the right team.

Set engineering and analytics standards on metadata, lineage, data quality, and audit controls across the Data Analytics Office, and architected the Field Inventory Report that became the data-lineage standard for the Strategic Data Warehouse.

items.title: Assistant Business Controller
bullets: Led cross-functional analytics and reporting modernization with HR, Sales, Marketing, Finance, and executive leadership, feeding operational and financial strategy at the regional leadership level.
Designed automated KPI dashboards and reporting frameworks that improved data accessibility and consistency, and established the data-governance and QA practices the team built on.
Picked and managed outside vendors and consultants on analytics, reporting, and finance-systems projects, with ownership of scope, timelines, and delivery quality.

groups.title: Analytics and Modeling: classification machine learning, ARIMA and time-series forecasting, statistical outlier and anomaly detection, data mining, applied econometrics, Python ML stack (pandas, NumPy, scikit-learn, statsmodels), Databricks ML
items: Languages and Platforms: Python, SQL, R, advanced Excel and VBA, Databricks lakehouse, Delta Lake, Spark, enterprise data warehousing, cloud data platforms (Azure-leaning), ETL and ELT pipelines
Reporting: Power BI, Tableau, SSRS, written and oral briefing for senior leadership
